source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure to Conduct Security Assessments

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure to Conduct Security Assessments
input_row.description: Periodically perform security assessments, including vulnerability assessments and penetration testing, to identify and address any security weaknesses. Review your security configuration against best practices and industry standards.
input_row.rationale: This helps ensure that any vulnerabilities that might lie dormant be addressed promptly, which would reduce the risk of a malicious attack. Reviewing and making sure the security policies are authentic ensures the safety of the organization data.
input_row.audit: 1. Define the Scope of the Security Assessment • Clearly define the scope of the security assessment for your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Determine the objectives, areas of focus, and any specific compliance or security standards you must adhere to. 2. Review Security Documentation • Familiarize yourself with the AWS security best practices and documentation related to Amazon DocumentDB. • Review the AWS Shared Responsibility Model and understand the security controls provided by AWS. 3. Assess Network Security • Review the network security configuration of your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Ensure it is deployed within a secure Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with appropriate security groups and network access control lists (ACLs). • Validate that the network traffic to and from the cluster is appropriately restricted based on your security requirements. 4. Evaluate Encryption Configuration • Assess the encryption settings for your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Verify that encryption at rest is enabled and that the data stored in the cluster is encrypted. • Validate that encryption in transit is enforced, ensuring that all client connections to the cluster are encrypted using SSL/TLS. 5. Review Access Control Mechanisms • Evaluate the access control mechanisms implemented for your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Ensure that appropriate Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies and roles are in place to control access to the cluster. • Review user accounts and their privileges, and validate that multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enforced for administrative access. 6. Examine Audit Logging and Monitoring • Review the audit logging and monitoring configuration for your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Verify that audit logging is enabled, capturing relevant database activities and events. • Evaluate the monitoring setup using Amazon CloudWatch or other tools to detect unusual or suspicious activities. 7. Assess Backup and Disaster Recovery • Evaluate the backup and disaster recovery mechanisms in place for your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Verify that automated backups are enabled and configured with an appropriate retention period. • Validate that manual backups can be performed and restored successfully. 8. Perform Vulnerability Scanning and Penetration Testing (If Applicable) • If allowed and within the terms of service, perform vulnerability scanning and penetration testing on your Amazon DocumentDB cluster. • Conduct security assessments to identify any vulnerabilities or weaknesses that could be exploited. 9. Document Findings and Remediation Plan • Document the findings of your security assessment, including any identified vulnerabilities or areas of improvement. • Develop a remediation plan that addresses the identified issues and outlines the necessary actions to enhance the security posture of your DocumentDB cluster. 10. Implement Remediation Measures • Implement the necessary remediation measures based on your remediation plan. • Apply security patches, adjust configuration settings, and strengthen access controls as required. 11. Regularly Repeat the Security Assessment • Conduct regular security assessments on your Amazon DocumentDB cluster to ensure ongoing compliance and identify new risks or vulnerabilities. • Stay updated with security best practices and apply any relevant updates or patches to your cluster.
input_row.justification: While certain aspects of DocumentDB security posture (such as encryption settings, network configurations, IAM policies, and audit logging) can be programmatically checked using AWS APIs, the core of this control—conducting comprehensive security assessments, including vulnerability assessments and penetration testing—requires human judgment, scoping, and actions outside the reach of AWS APIs. These activities involve subjective evaluation, interpretation of results, and potentially testing application logic or business processes, which cannot be fully automated.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. **Define the Scope of the Security Assessment:** - Identify all Amazon DocumentDB clusters and related resources to be assessed. - Determine the objectives, compliance requirements, and areas of focus (e.g., network security, encryption, access controls). 2. **Review Security Documentation:** - Manually review AWS security best practices for DocumentDB and the AWS Shared Responsibility Model. - Access documentation at https://docs.aws.amazon.com/documentdb/latest/developerguide/security.html. 3. **Conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing:** - Schedule and perform vulnerability scans and penetration tests on DocumentDB endpoints, following AWS penetration testing policies. - Document findings and remediation actions. 4. **Review Security Configuration:** - Manually verify network settings, encryption, IAM policies, and audit logging via the AWS Console or CLI, ensuring they align with best practices and organizational policies. 5. **Document and Report:** - Record assessment scope, methods, findings, and remediation steps. - Review results with relevant stakeholders and update security posture as needed.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control emphasizes conducting comprehensive security assessments, including vulnerability assessments and penetration testing, which necessitates human judgment and cannot be fully automated through AWS APIs. While specific aspects of the DocumentDB security posture (like encryption, IAM roles, and network settings) can be queried via APIs, the overarching requirement to perform and interpret security assessments inherently leans towards manual execution due to the subjective nature of the tasks involved. Hence, this control will be categorized as Manual.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Define the Scope of the Security Assessment:**
   - Identify all Amazon DocumentDB clusters and related resources to be assessed.
   - Determine the objectives, compliance requirements, and areas of focus (e.g., network security, encryption, access controls).

2. **Review Security Documentation:**
   - Manually review AWS security best practices for DocumentDB and the AWS Shared Responsibility Model.
   - Access documentation at [AWS DocumentDB Security Best Practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/documentdb/latest/developerguide/security.html).

3. **Assess Network Security:**
   - In the AWS Console, navigate to the DocumentDB section and select your cluster.
   - Review the VPC settings and ensure that the cluster is deployed within a secure Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with appropriate security groups and network access control lists (ACLs).
   - Validate that the network traffic to and from the cluster is restricted based on your security requirements.

4. **Evaluate Encryption Configuration:**
   - Inspect the encryption settings in the AWS Console for your DocumentDB cluster.
   - Verify that encryption at rest is enabled and that the data stored in the cluster is encrypted.
   - Ensure that encryption in transit is enforced by checking the cluster settings and SSL/TLS configuration.

5. **Review Access Control Mechanisms:**
   - Evaluate IAM policies by going to the IAM section in the AWS Console.
   - Review the access controls to ensure appropriate permissions are granted to users and applications accessing the DocumentDB cluster.
   - Confirm that multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enforced for any administrative access.

6. **Examine Audit Logging and Monitoring:**
   - In DocumentDB settings, ensure that audit logging is enabled.
   - Check that relevant activities and events are being captured.
   - Validate the monitoring setup using Amazon CloudWatch to detect unusual or suspicious activities.

7. **Assess Backup and Disaster Recovery:**
   - Review the backup settings in the AWS Console and confirm that automated backups are enabled with an appropriate retention period.
   - Perform a manual check to ensure that manual backups can also be performed and restored successfully.

8. **Conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing:**
   - Schedule and perform vulnerability scans and penetration tests on DocumentDB endpoints, adhering to AWS penetration testing policies.
   - Document findings and remediation actions taken.

9. **Document Findings and Remediation Plan:**
   - Keep a record of the assessment findings, vulnerabilities, or areas for improvement.
   - Develop a remediation plan to address identified issues and outline actions to enhance security posture.

10. **Implement Remediation Measures:**
    - Apply the necessary remediation measures as identified in the remediation plan, including configuration adjustments and security patch applications.

11. **Regularly Repeat the Security Assessment:**
    - Establish a schedule for conducting regular security assessments to ensure ongoing compliance and to identify new risks or vulnerabilities.
    - Stay updated with security best practices and apply relevant updates or patches to the cluster as needed.
